title: phegopteris hypolepioides
intro: Phegopteris hypolepioides, also known as the autumn fern or autumn maiden fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to East Asia and is used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and has been found to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Phegopteris hypolepioides can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
